trade.summary: Solid bilateral trade. Switzerland exports pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and machinery. Sweden exports machinery, vehicles, paper products, and IT services. Both countries are advanced technology exporters with complementary specializations.

diplomatic.summary: Excellent relations between two prosperous, progressive European democracies. Both strongly support multilateralism, international law, and human rights. Sweden joined NATO in 2024, while Switzerland remains neutral—a growing divergence in security posture, though values alignment remains high. Cooperation in UN, WTO, and other multilateral bodies.

military.summary: Switzerland is neutral; Sweden is now a NATO member (2024). This creates some divergence in security alignment. Previously both maintained neutrality, but Sweden's NATO accession changes the dynamic. No formal military cooperation. Informal security dialogue.

financial.summary: Strong bilateral investment. Swedish companies (Volvo, Ericsson, IKEA-related) have Swiss operations. Swiss pharmaceuticals and financial firms are active in Sweden. Stockholm and Zurich are both major financial centers.

energy.summary: Sweden exports hydroelectric and nuclear power through European grids that benefit Switzerland. Cooperation on Nordic-European energy integration. Sweden's wood pellet exports and bioenergy contribute to Swiss energy mix.

scientific.summary: Very strong scientific collaboration. ETH Zurich and Swedish universities (KTH, Chalmers, Karolinska) maintain deep partnerships. Joint research in life sciences, climate, and materials science. High joint publication rate.
